title: Freehand: the skeleton, from memory
why.empty: The editor is empty. I know that is unpleasant. Write one tag — any tag. Then the next one. Nobody types out a whole page at once.
why.no-doctype: The line at the top is missing. The one that shows nothing but tells the browser which rules to read by. Without it the browser guesses.
why.no-html: There is no wrapper. Everything on the page lives inside one tag — it starts after the first line and ends at the very bottom.
why.no-head: There is no head. That is the part a person never sees but the browser reads: the tab name, the character set, the links to files.
why.no-title: There is no name in the tab. Open any site and look at the top — something is written there. On yours it will show the file path.
why.no-body: There is no body. That is everything a person actually sees. Without it the page is a wrapper with nothing in it.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order. Check backwards from the end: the last one opened is the first one closed.
blocks.0.text: Empty editor. No hints in the code. No dashes to fill in.
blocks.2.text: Write the whole page for your topic — from scratch, from memory. The skeleton, the name in the tab, the line at the very top, the heading and a few sentences.
blocks.3.text: Do not go back and copy from earlier lessons. Try on your own first. You will get something wrong — that is part of the exercise.
blocks.5.text: There is a difference between "I understand it when I see it" and "I can write it". The first is a feeling. The second is a skill. Only one of them will help you in front of an empty file on a Monday morning.
blocks.6.text: If you manage without looking back — you know the skeleton. If not — go back, look, and try again tomorrow. That is not failure, that is how memory works.
